html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

elements: green polygonal surface
black curved edge line
normal vector n with components (n_x, n_y)
tangent vector t perpendicular to n
right angle marker between n and t

interactions.teaches: Dragging the vectors allows you to see how the normal and tangent interact at different orientations along the edge.

demo_steps.title: Initial vector setup
demo_steps.narration: Here, the normal vector n is shown perpendicular to the edge of this 2D shape. The tangent vector t is also drawn, perpendicular to n, lying along the edge.

demo_steps.title: Understanding tangent direction
demo_steps.narration: The tangent vector t dictates the direction along the edge. Notice how it remains perpendicular to n, meaning any change along t keeps Y constant, demonstrating ∂Y/∂t = 0.

demo_steps.title: Vectors in motion
demo_steps.narration: By adjusting the orientation of n and t, you can see how their directions are maintained relative to each other. This interaction is crucial for understanding their roles in differentiating along the edge.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Top-down orthographic view encompassing the entire 2D shape and both vectors originating from the edge
interactionPlan.notes: Ensure vectors are updated in real-time as they are dragged, maintaining their perpendicular relationship.
